counter_adjustments.reason: gauntlet_v1 factory prereg (docs/lab_prereg_gauntlet_v1.md section 8): 90 pre-committed grid cells = 90 hypotheses; new_strategy added +1, this adjustment adds the remaining +89. Survivor child slugs will each carry a -1 adjustment at registration so no cell is ever counted twice.

migrated_from.note: house-lab generalization (operator directive): single registry + single multiple-testing counter; pre-migration file frozen as history

bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: 3bps slippage (tighter than gauntlet_v1's LARGE-bucket 5bps, disclosed judgment call for highly liquid ETFs), $25 min ticket, $10k notional. Prereg-mandated 2x-slippage (6bps) sensitivity on the sole stage-1 survivor (sma_200): IS CAGR 1.03% (still beats benchmark's 0.08%), HO CAGR 1.11% (still loses to benchmark's 3.03%) -- verdict unchanged at 2x.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: signal_lag=1 (identical engine feature/discipline as gauntlet_v1): the selection rule sees bars only through the trading day before the execution day; trades fill at the execution day's own close. No fundamentals, no restatement exposure -- signals are pure price (252-day return, 200-day SMA).
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: 2 trials by design, both counted: hypotheses_ever went from 92 to 93 at prereg (new_strategy +1, cell-count adjustment +1 -- one count per grid cell, same convention as gauntlet_v1). DSR benchmarked against n_trials=2 with variance_of_sr=0.01796 measured on the grid's own 2 in-sample Sharpes. Holdout touched exactly once, by the sole stage-1 survivor (sma_200).
bias_checklist.overfitting: Zero free parameters after the prereg commit: the 252-day and 200-day lookback windows are the canonical textbook values from the cited literature (Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen; Delphi's own 200-day-class MA convention), not tuned to this dataset. Portfolio construction (equal weight among currently-long assets, no top-N) has no free knob. abs_mom_252 failed stage 1 outright; sma_200 cleared stage 1 but then failed stage 2 against the SAME frozen benchmark bar -- the holdout did its job.
bias_checklist.regime: In-sample (2008-08..2017-12) spans the GFC recovery and the low-rate QE decade, where the always-invested benchmark barely made money (0.08%/yr CAGR) and a defensive trend filter had room to add value (sma_200 IS CAGR 1.21%). Holdout (2018-01..2026-06) includes the 2018 vol spike, COVID crash, and 2022 bond bear market, but ALSO a strong multi-year recovery that the always-invested benchmark captured in full (HO CAGR 3.03%) while sma_200's periodic cash exposure captured only part of it (HO CAGR 1.28%) -- the same 'a passive benchmark just did better in this decade' pattern that killed gauntlet_v1's low-vol survivors, now observed in a structurally different asset class and signal family.
bias_checklist.selection: Fixed, named 17-ETF universe (one liquid instrument per rates/credit/commodity/currency/country-equity slot) chosen for category coverage and inception history BEFORE any return was examined -- disclosed as a judgment call, not a performance-driven pick. The 2-cell grid (abs_mom_252, sma_200) was the complete pre-committed catalog; nothing added or removed after the prereg.
bias_checklist.small_n: 215 monthly rebalances 2008-08..2026-06 (~112 in-sample, ~102 holdout); ~2,348 IS and ~2,134 HO daily equity-curve observations per cell for Sharpe/DSR/PSR, using each cell's own n_obs, skew and kurtosis. mean_excess (-0.24%/yr) and shrunk mean (-0.02%/yr, n=2) both sit near zero, honestly reflecting one cell failing badly (abs_mom_252) and one cell narrowly failing only at the holdout stage (sma_200) rather than a uniform result.
bias_checklist.survivorship: Fixed, currently-live, liquid 17-ETF universe -- no delistings in this window (disclosed in the prereg as a scope choice). A real infrastructure bug WAS found and fixed before any signal was computed: Robinhood's historicals endpoint pads dates before a symbol's real listing with a flat, constant-OHLC placeholder bar instead of returning not_found (confirmed on SHY: 418 identical 81.05 bars, volume 0, then a real 2.44M-volume jump on its true 2007-08-31 data-start date). Mechanically stripped per-symbol (open==high==low==close run from bar 0) before any lookback signal was computed -- verified against EEM/FXI, which show genuine day-to-day price variation despite occasional volume=0 records, so the strip correctly left their real history untouched. Now a reusable house utility: shared.historicals.strip_preinception_padding.

backtest.notes: 2-cell grid on a fixed 17-ETF macro universe. Stage 1 (in-sample 2008-08..2017-12): abs_mom_252 DSR=0.00 (fails, also loses to benchmark IS CAGR: -1.48% vs 0.08%); sma_200 DSR=1.00 (passes) and beats benchmark IS CAGR (1.21% vs 0.08%) -- advances alone. Stage 2 (holdout 2018-01..2026-06, once): sma_200 PSR=1.00 (genuinely positive risk-adjusted return) but HO CAGR 1.28% does NOT beat the benchmark's 3.03% -- zero stage-2 passers. Unchanged at 2x slippage. Also produced a reusable house infrastructure fix: shared.historicals.strip_preinception_padding, guarding against Robinhood's flat pre-listing placeholder bars.

cross_asset_trend.falsifiable_claim: At least one of 2 pre-committed cells (252-day absolute momentum; 200-day SMA trend filter) clears DSR>=0.95 (n_trials=2) and beats the always-invested EW benchmark in-sample, then clears PSR>=0.95 and beats the benchmark on the untouched 2018-2026 holdout. Zero passers at either stage refutes.
cross_asset_trend.mechanism: Absolute (time-series) trend-following on macro asset-class ETFs (rates, credit, commodities, currencies, country equity indices): persistent one-directional order flow from non-forecasting actors (pension glide-path rebalancers, corporate hedgers, central banks defending currency bands, sovereign commodity allocators) creates autocorrelation a simple trend rule can ride. Structurally distinct from gauntlet_v1 cross-sectional equity momentum (refuted), whose proposed source was single-company analyst underreaction, not asset-class order flow.

prereg.metric: Net-of-cost monthly-rebalanced daily equity curve per cell (signal_lag=1, execution at next month's first trading-day close); annualized Sharpe -> DSR (n_trials=2) in-sample; PSR on holdout; net CAGR vs the equal-weight always-invested 17-ETF benchmark.
prereg.population_definition: Fixed 17-ETF universe (TLT, IEF, SHY, LQD, HYG, GLD, SLV, USO, DBA, DBB, UUP, FXE, FXY, EEM, EFA, FXI, EWZ) chosen for asset-class category coverage and long inception history, not performance -- one per rates/credit/commodity/currency/country-equity slot. Vol-linked ETPs excluded by design (structural roll-decay confound). No PIT ranking; fixed named list, disclosed judgment call.
prereg.success_criteria: Stage 1 (IS): DSR>=0.95 @ n_trials=2 AND beat EW benchmark IS CAGR -- advance. Stage 2 (holdout, once): PSR>=0.95 AND beat benchmark holdout CAGR -- supported. Refuted iff zero passers at either stage. Inconclusive only for >20% rebalance coverage failure.

cross_asset_trend.underutilized_because: Retail/self-directed capital concentrates almost entirely on single-stock picking (as every prior house study -- Oracle, Midas, Gauntlet -- also did); a 17-ETF absolute-trend overlay requires no stock-picking skill and is mechanically trivial, yet is rarely run by small accounts who default to buy-and-hold or single-name bets. Professional CTAs run this with leverage and dozens of futures contracts unavailable to a long-only, no-leverage retail account -- this tests the stripped-down, house-constrained version.
cross_asset_trend.who_loses: Non-forecasting flow: fuel hedgers locking costs regardless of price view, pensions shifting to bonds on a fixed schedule regardless of rate outlook, central banks defending currency bands, sovereign wealth funds rebalancing commodity allocations on policy schedules rather than return forecasts.

bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: Per-bucket linear slippage (LARGE 5bps, SMALL 25bps), $25 min ticket, $3 price floor, $1M median-21d-dollar-volume floor, at $10k/cell -- house scale, where these names are tradable. Prereg-mandated 2x-slippage disclosure on the five holdout cells: vol_L126__N10__LARGE: IS 9.15%/HO 8.52% at 2x slippage; vol_L126__N25__LARGE: IS 9.27%/HO 6.69% at 2x slippage; vol_L126__N50__LARGE: IS 9.48%/HO 8.55% at 2x slippage; vol_L126__N50__SMALL: IS 8.85%/HO 4.13% at 2x slippage; vol_L63__N25__LARGE: IS 8.81%/HO 8.76% at 2x slippage. Verdicts unchanged at 2x.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Signals computed from raw closeadj through the month-end signal close t; execution at the NEXT month's first trading close via explicit signal/execution date pairs (engine signal_lag feature, added before any run; carry-forward fills price executions but can never satisfy signal-eligibility windows). Marketcap dated t derives from t's close and share count -- used for t's signal, traded t+1. No fundamentals, no restatement exposure.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: 90 trials by design, all counted: hypotheses_ever went 1 -> 91 at prereg time (house counter, registry adjustment record). DSR benchmarked against n_trials=90 with cross-sectional variance_of_sr=0.0556 measured on the grid's own 90 in-sample Sharpes. The holdout was touched exactly once, by the top-5 only. No cell beyond the 90 was ever computed.
bias_checklist.overfitting: Zero free parameters after the prereg commit: windows, N, buckets, floors, costs, splits and bars all frozen before data. 186 monthly rebalances and ~3,900 daily observations per cell in-sample; the <=5-survivor cap bounded selection intensity on the 120-month holdout. The refutation itself is the overfitting demonstration: 10 cells cleared a deflated in-sample bar and all 5 advancing cells failed out-of-sample against the benchmark.
bias_checklist.regime: In-sample 2000-2015 contains the dot-com crash and GFC; holdout 2016-2025 is one long bull with a fast COVID crash. Prereg section 9.7 named clearing both as the regime test; low-vol passed the crash-heavy sample and failed the bull decade against the benchmark -- the refutation IS the regime finding: the family's in-sample edge was crash exposure, not persistent alpha.
bias_checklist.selection: Population = every name passing the prereg's mechanical screens on each of 186 IS + 120 HO month-ends (no hand-picking; catalog saved as house population gauntlet_v1_universes, 612 rows with coverage note). The grid itself was the complete pre-committed catalog of variants: 90 cells enumerated in the prereg before any panel pull, none added or removed after.
bias_checklist.small_n: Per cell: ~3,900 IS daily returns, 186 IS + 120 HO monthly rebalances; DSR/PSR computed with each cell's own n_obs, skew and kurtosis. Factory-level mean_excess is the mean IS CAGR gap vs bucket benchmark across all 90 cells (-5.5%/yr, 14/90 positive); shrinkage on n=90 leaves the sign untouched. No forward test follows -- only a supported backtest earns one.
bias_checklist.survivorship: Sharadar SEP/DAILY panels include delisted names through their final trading day (verified SIVBQ/BBBYQ); universes built per-date from names alive that day. 27.2M IS bars across 14,240 tickers, 0 skipped. DAILY-vs-SEP mismatch: median 3 names/date (0.06%, max 16) excluded and disclosed. Delisting exits sellable at stale final close at next monthly rebalance -- optimistic, which can only have UNDERSTATED the refuted families' failure.

backtest.notes: Factory-level record: n=90 grid cells; mean_excess = mean in-sample CAGR excess vs same-bucket EW benchmark across the grid. Stage 1 killed 80/90 (all momentum/reversal/size/neglect); stage 2 (one holdout pass, top-5 low-vol) killed the rest: PSR vs zero = 1.0 for all five, but none beat the bucket benchmark 2016-2025. Per-cell data: docs/data/gauntlet_v1/.

gauntlet_v1.falsifiable_claim: At least one of the 90 pre-committed cells clears the frozen in-sample bar (DSR >= 0.95 with n_trials=90 AND net CAGR above the matching equal-weight bucket benchmark) and then the frozen holdout bar (PSR >= 0.95 AND beats the benchmark out-of-sample, net of costs). Zero passers at either stage refutes the factory.
gauntlet_v1.mechanism: Factory-level meta-hypothesis: the best-documented cross-sectional equity anomalies — momentum (investor underreaction and slow information diffusion), short-term reversal (compensation for providing liquidity into price pressure), low-volatility (leverage-constrained investors overpaying for lottery-like high-vol names), and size/neglect (institutional mandates and analyst coverage thinning out below the top ~1000 names, leaving mispricings unharvested) — are each claimed by decades of academic literature to persist in US equities. Each grid cell is the simplest implementable long-only form of one such claim. The factory tests whether ANY survive realistic costs, a 16y in-sample, a 10y holdout, and deflation for 90 simultaneous tries at the house's own scale.

prereg.metric: Net-of-cost daily equity curve per cell (signals at month-end close t, execution at close of first trading day of the next month, per-bucket slippage); annualized Sharpe -> deflated Sharpe (n_trials=90) in-sample, PSR on holdout; net CAGR vs matching equal-weight bucket benchmark.
prereg.population_definition: Monthly point-in-time universes from the full Sharadar panel (SEP bars + DAILY marketcap, delisted names included through final trading day), 1998-12-01 to 2025-12-31: at each month-end signal date, names with both a DAILY marketcap and SEP bar, unadjusted close >= $3, median 21d dollar volume >= $1M; LARGE = marketcap ranks 1-500, SMALL = ranks 501-2000. Ranks 2001+ out of scope by design. Full construction rules frozen in the prereg doc, section 3.
prereg.success_criteria: Stage 1 (all 90 cells, in-sample executions 2000-07..2015-12): survive iff DSR >= 0.95 AND net CAGR > bucket benchmark; top 5 survivors by DSR advance. Stage 2 (holdout executions 2016-01..2025-12, touched once): pass iff PSR vs 0 >= 0.95 AND net CAGR > benchmark holdout CAGR. Factory supported iff >=1 stage-2 passer; refuted iff zero at either stage; inconclusive only for >20% coverage failure. Survivors then face the standard >=20-grade paper forward gate on shrunk mean.

gauntlet_v1.underutilized_because: Not underutilized in the aggregate — these are famous factors — but famously fragile after costs, in long-only implementable form, at small size, outside backtest samples. The factory's point is precisely to measure how much of the published effect is harvestable by THIS house, and to build the graveyard of cells that are not.
gauntlet_v1.who_loses: Momentum: late reactors who anchor on stale prices. Reversal: impatient liquidity demanders forced to sell into weakness. Low-vol: leverage-constrained lottery buyers overpaying for volatile names. Size/neglect: nobody is on the other side per se — the edge, if real, is a premium for holding what mandates and coverage ignore.

bias_checklist.costs_liquidity: Not formally modeled (no spread/slippage deduction), but this does not affect the verdict since the raw effect is already negative -- costs would only push it further negative. Many names show extreme reverse-split-adjusted price levels (e.g. VCIG entry/exit in the millions post-adjustment), a tell that this population skews toward chronically distressed, thinly-traded microcaps even before accounting for the 15 that vanished from the data entirely.
bias_checklist.look_ahead: Entry fixed at trading-day 185 from each name's own first trade -- a count-based rule using only information knowable at IPO time (the 180-day lockup term is boilerplate stated in the S-1/424B4 before day 0). No name was included or excluded based on anything that happened after its own IPO date; SPY's return uses the identical calendar dates as each stock's own entry/exit, not a shared window.
bias_checklist.multiple_testing: This is hypothesis #1 in lab['hypotheses_ever'] -- the first strategy ever registered in this lab. No prior variant of this idea was tried against this or any other dataset before this cut.
bias_checklist.overfitting: Two knobs total (185-trading-day entry offset, 60-trading-day hold), both fixed by the mechanism (lockup length + a round-number hold) in the prereg before any data was seen. Neither knob was adjusted after seeing the result -- this is the first and only cut on this design; a redesign requires a new slug and fresh prereg.
bias_checklist.regime: Single sample: 2023 IPO class held into 2024, a disinflation/soft-landing rally regime for SPY (each name's own SPY window returned +2% to +19%). Even against a rising market the median small-cap IPO fell -33% over the post-lockup 60-day window -- this looks like the well-documented long-run small-cap-IPO underperformance continuing past the lockup date, not a distinct reversal at it. Untested in a down-market regime, but the negative sign does not obviously depend on this being a bull-market sample.
bias_checklist.selection: Population built from the complete public 2023 IPO calendar (154 rows, stockanalysis.com), filtered by mechanical rules (SPAC-name pattern, spinoff, foreign cross-listing, missing price, price>$10) applied uniformly before any return was computed. IPO price<=$10 was used as an operational proxy for the prereg's '<$300M offer size' criterion rather than per-name S-1 totals -- verifying exact offer size for ~150 candidates individually would have cost as many tool calls as the rest of the study, for a threshold that already cleanly separates the list (obscure microcaps vs. the year's recognized large IPOs, no ambiguous middle case observed).
bias_checklist.small_n: Raw n=65, above the prereg's n>=30 floor. Shrunk mean (prior_n=20, prior_mean=0) is -8.01%, close to the raw -10.41% since n=65 dominates the prior -- shrinkage does not rescue this toward zero in any meaningful way. No forward test follows, since only a 'supported' backtest earns one under proteus/lab.py.
bias_checklist.survivorship: 15/80 (18.75%) of the population could not be priced because the broker's historicals API does not serve bars for tickers now inactive/renamed/delisted, regardless of whether real trading data existed during the 2024 entry/exit window. Of 5 spot-checked via WebSearch, all 5 were distress outcomes (Nasdaq delisting-for-cause, forced reverse splits, business-combination exits, sub-$1 collapse) -- meaning the 65-name result almost certainly OVERSTATES the true population mean excess, not understates it. A free alternate data source (stooq.com) was unreachable (503 via proxy); stockanalysis.com's history page redirects a renamed ticker to its successor's current price series rather than the pre-rename history.

backtest.notes: Median excess -33.07%, win rate 18/65=27.7%, t=-0.62. Result is dominated in sign only by a single +1011.53% biotech outlier (BMR) that exceeds the entire net excess in magnitude -- excluding it, the other 64 names average roughly -25.9% excess. Fails the supported-criterion 'no single event >40% of total excess' on its own even before considering the negative mean.

ipo_lockup_reversion.falsifiable_claim: A mechanical, non-discretionary long entry exactly 185 trading days after IPO pricing (5 sessions after the standard 180-day lockup expires) across a complete population of small-cap (sub-$300M offer size) US IPOs, held 60 trading days, produces mean excess return vs SPY that is reliably positive (t>=1.5, n>=30); if mean excess is at or below zero, or the raw n is unreachable/inconclusive, the hypothesis is refuted or inconclusive as tested.
ipo_lockup_reversion.mechanism: Standard IPO underwriting agreements impose a ~180-day lockup restricting pre-IPO holders (founders, employees, VC/PE funds) from selling. This is a fixed, calendar-certain, information-free event: on expiry, tradeable float mechanically increases as previously-restricted shares become sellable, independent of any change in the issuer's fundamentals. Anticipatory selling and short-side positioning into the well-known 'lockup cliff' narrative depresses price into and just after expiry as a pure supply/liquidity shock, not a valuation reassessment. Because the shock carries no new information, once the initial wave of lockup-driven selling is absorbed by the market (typically within 1-2 weeks), price should partially revert toward its pre-shock trend as the temporary oversupply clears and the stock re-prices on fundamentals rather than the float shock.

prereg.metric: Simple holding-period return minus SPY's simple return over the same calendar dates ('excess return'), entry T+185 trading days, 60-trading-day hold.
prereg.population_definition: All US primary equity IPOs (common stock, standard underwritten IPO; excludes SPACs, closed-end funds, ADRs, uplistings, direct listings) that priced in calendar year 2023, with total offer size < $300M, sourced from a complete public IPO calendar for that year (not a hand-picked subset). Delisted/failed names are included, not dropped.
prereg.success_criteria: Supported: n>=30, mean excess > 0, t>=1.5, no single event >40% of total excess. Refuted: mean excess<=0, or t<1.0. Else inconclusive.

ipo_lockup_reversion.underutilized_because: Lockup-expiry research and financial media coverage concentrate almost entirely on famous, mega-cap IPOs (exactly the kind session 3 rejected on EQPT/SPCX as already-published and priced-in); the broad population of small-cap, low-coverage IPOs has calendar-certain, EDGAR-documented lockup dates that are public but that no systematic screener or analyst desk tracks, leaving the effect unarbitraged specifically in names too small and unglamorous to attract the attention that kills it in famous names.
ipo_lockup_reversion.who_loses: Momentum shorts and lockup-cliff narrative traders who pile onto the anticipated overhang and are then squeezed as selling exhausts and price reverts; also pre-IPO holders forced to sell into a temporarily depressed, oversupplied tape rather than at a fundamentals-clearing price.

quiet_cluster_ghost.falsifiable_claim: Quiet-cluster mean 12-month excess (vs IWM) > 0 with t>=2 on n>=30 graded events validates; mean excess <=0 or t<2 with n>=30 refutes; n<30 stays inconclusive. Fresh Form 4 population from 2026-06-01 onward only (spent replay data excluded by construction).
quiet_cluster_ghost.mechanism: Backlog #1: insider clusters that fire with no contemporaneous 8-K, no abnormal price reaction, and no volume anomaly at the knowability date may carry more private conviction than clusters the market already reacted to at filing -- the raw (unfiltered) cluster population measured -6.4%/yr at 12mo in the spent 2025-01-01..2026-05-31 replay; this tests whether the quiet subset differs.

prereg.metric: 12-month (and interim 6-month) excess return vs IWM per quiet-cluster event, entry at first close on/after knowability date+5 calendar days.
prereg.population_definition: Every Form 4 open-market-buy cluster (>=2 distinct insiders, each >=$10k, aggregate >=$50k, 60-calendar-day window, grant-mill >60-filing issuers excluded) filed EDGAR-wide from 2026-06-01 onward, filtered to the subset with no 8-K within +/-3 trading days of knowability, |return|<2% on knowability day and next day, and volume <1.5x trailing-21d median on knowability day.
prereg.success_criteria: Validated: mean 12mo excess>0, t>=2, n>=30. Refuted: mean excess<=0 or t<2 at n>=30. Inconclusive: n<30 -- still accruing, no early verdict.

quiet_cluster_ghost.underutilized_because: The full cluster signal is public (every Form 4 is EDGAR-visible) but almost nobody filters clusters by the ABSENCE of contemporaneous news/price/volume signal, since that requires a three-way negative screen most scanners are not built to run at all; if the quiet subset differs, it is underused because it is operationally annoying to isolate, not because it is secret.
quiet_cluster_ghost.who_loses: If real: market participants who only act on visible catalysts (news, volume, price pops) and structurally miss the quiet subset until the re-rating catches up -- the same late-reactor story as momentum, but at the filing-conviction layer rather than the price layer.
